text: I arrive as engineering manager evaluating team collaboration and security. My past: My team already uses several tools, and every platform decision creates migration and compliance risk.
Today I want to: Decide whether GitHub feels credible enough for a team evaluation.
My life goal is: Protect my team's delivery reputation by choosing stable engineering systems.
I have 100 life-years. Each meaningful action costs 1 month(s); each committed dollar costs 1 year(s).

label: arrival
title: GitHub · Change is constant. GitHub keeps you ahead. · GitHub

text: I see "GitHub · Change is constant. GitHub keeps you ahead. · GitHub".
I think: I see GitHub positioning itself as a steady place for constant change, but I need substance before trusting it for my team.
My reaction: My first reaction is cautiously interested; the brand feels credible, but the visible evidence is still thin.
Idea: show security, collaboration, and enterprise stability proof immediately for leaders making platform decisions.
Life-cost decision: Spending 1 month of life is worth it if the next step quickly clarifies whether this protects my team's delivery reputation.
Truth assessment: My private judgment is that GitHub starts with strong credibility, but I am only moderately confident until I see concrete team and security details.
